title: A Smile For The Witnesses
dramatic_function: removes stable bargaining from the story

signifier_package.room_logic: violence is meant to be read as a message

dialogue_grammar.speech_pattern: sounds playful in ways that make the room feel spiritually contaminated

narrative_payoff.resolution: victory means making it out alive without accepting the villain's worldview

escalation_pattern: welcomes disaster because it proves the worldview
opening_image: By the time the hero understands the villain's logic, the villain has already made that logic feel normal.
sample_lines: "I orchestrate a spectacle; you just watch the applause of your own dread."
"Every scar on this floor is a story I wrote—let me read yours next."
"Who needs a weapon when every glance can ignite a blaze?"
hooks: The villain seeks to turn inevitable ruin into an act of art, demanding the audience applaud the collapse.
Their real power lies in scripting fear as a civic duty rather than a threat.
They only reveal their true menace once society has already signed onto their manifesto.
